shape_semantics: I2S-ANTI. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,W,_,W: cmplog BLOCKS, while value_profile AND naive RESOLVE (vpc non-decisive). The technique that HURTS is I2S (cmplog's input-to-state substitution): the branch is reached by plain havoc byte/token exploration (naive) and by the gradient (value_profile), but cmplog's extra I2S stage diverts or pollutes the corpus and BLOCKS it. The shape (Layer 1) already asserts I2S-anti (cmplog is the sole loser). The evidence test does NOT re-confirm that cmplog blocks (that would verify the loser-pattern with the loser-pattern, violating G2); it independently discriminates WHY I2S hurts on each branch -- whether the I2S stage (a) actively splices a WRONG/competing operand that DEPLETES the cmplog corpus of the target byte region (a measurable corpus signature on cmplog vs naive, INDEPENDENT of whether cmplog blocks), or (b) has NO harvestable input-resident operand at all (the gate value is table/state/property-derived) so I2S is pure overhead, or (c) the gate is a length/sequence-accumulation gate that cmplog's CMP-biased budget SHORTENS the input away from.

hypotheses.label: I2S splices a WRONG/competing but abundant CMP operand into the input (a well-known font-table tag, a script-tag/codepoint constant, an in-range fixed-point magnitude, or a competing protocol value), pinning the cmplog corpus onto that decoy and DEPLETING it of the target byte value at the winner gate's offset; naive/value_profile never harvest the decoy so they freely reach the target

params.comment: For the branch's W/L gate offset (the divergent byte from the analysis byte-diff / per-role overlay), compute the cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment of the TARGET byte value (the winner-resolving value at that offset) vs the DECOY value (the competing operand I2S substitutes). Anti-direction: cmplog DEPLETES the target (signed_target_enrich < 0) and ENRICHES the decoy (decoy_enrich > 0).
params.target_value_source: signature.operand_literal (e.g. DFontTag 00 00 01 00 for 5391; 0x0E1B/0x0E1D/0x0E1F for 5954; PNG_FIXED_ERROR overflow byte for libpng/3977; 0x3a for openthread/13349,13391; script/tag constants for 5723/5881) at the cmp-site offset from the per-role overlay

hypotheses.prediction: On the gate offset the cmplog corpus is DEPLETED of the target value relative to naive (signed_target_enrich < -0.3) AND/OR ENRICHED in the decoy operand (decoy_enrich > 0): I2S is actively steering bytes AWAY from the winner value. A pure-overhead mechanism (I2S has no operand to substitute) would predict signed_target_enrich ~ 0 and operand_enrichment returning no_gate_signature -- no directional depletion, because there is no decoy to splice. A pro-I2S mechanism would predict signed_target_enrich > 0. Only the active-decoy-substitution mechanism predicts a NEGATIVE target enrichment / positive decoy enrichment, which is what distinguishes 'I2S splices the wrong abundant operand' from 'I2S simply has nothing to do here'.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate needs havoc to BUILD or PRESERVE a long / periodically-repeated glyph-record byte sequence (multi-matra record runs, long multi-glyph Thai state-machine traversal); the gate value is table/state-derived (no harvestable operand), but cmplog's CMP-biased budget produces SHORTER, heterogeneous inputs that never reach the required sequence length, while naive's byte-expansion/copy operators grow and sustain it

params.comment: Reuse joint_necessity's struct-size half: take the median seed SIZE of the winner-resolving (naive/value_profile) seed set vs the cmplog-BLOCKING seed set. size_lift = median(resolving_size) / median(blocking_size). The length/accumulation subtype predicts the resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the blocking seeds. --tokens is unused for this hypothesis (size only); the token-count half of the tool is ignored here.

tokens_by_target.harfbuzz: (size-only; token vocabulary not used for this hypothesis)

hypotheses.prediction: The winner-resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the cmplog-blocking seeds (size_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is driven by sequence/length accumulation that cmplog's CMP-biased, shorter inputs fail to build. A no-operand-overhead mechanism (subtype 3 below) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 -- cmplog and naive reach SIMILARLY-sized inputs and the gate is simply a derived category cmplog can't aim at, with no length asymmetry. A decoy-substitution mechanism would have a fixed-offset operand signature (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature). This length asymmetry, on a gate with NO harvestable operand, is what separates 'I2S shortens the input below the accumulation threshold' from plain 'I2S overhead on a derived gate'.

hypotheses.label: The gate value is computed from shaping tables, Unicode-property lookups, Ragel state-machine internals, or derived category classification -- NOT a literal value compared against input bytes anywhere. I2S has no input-resident CMP operand to harvest or substitute; its per-execution callback overhead and misdirected substitution budget crowd out the broad havoc byte exploration that resolves the branch, with NO directional corpus depletion and NO length asymmetry

params.comment: operand_enrichment is asked for a gate signature; on these branches it returns no_gate_signature because the gate is NOT a fixed-offset value comparison (it is a derived/table/state category). The DIAGNOSTIC for this hypothesis is the ABSENCE of a harvestable operand (skip == no_gate_signature) COMBINED WITH the absence of a length asymmetry (the i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation hypothesis, tried first, did NOT fire). This is the residual / catch-all I2S-anti subtype.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset operand to harvest) AND there is no resolving-vs-blocking length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis is ruled out): I2S simply has nothing to substitute and is pure overhead. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead yield a fixed-offset operand with negative target enrichment (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature, signed_target_enrich < 0). A sequence-accumulation mechanism would yield size_lift >= 1.3. The conjunction no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3 is exactly what isolates 'derived gate, no operand, no length effect -- just I2S overhead' from the two operand/length subtypes.

notes: COLLAPSE / MERGE record: 31 branches, 7 prior-hint clusters collapsed to 3 discriminable hypotheses, guided by the Leg-1 operand_enrichment empirical run. (1) DECOY-SUBSTITUTION / TARGET-DEPLETION absorbs FIVE prior clusters that the data cannot tell apart -- i2s_font_tag_corpus_pollution (5391/5815/5843/5857/5931: SFNT/table tags spliced in), i2s_misdirects_from_protocol_byte (openthread 13349/13391: competing protocol value vs 0x3a), i2s_steers_toward_valid_range_blocks_error_sentinel (libpng/3977: in-range fixed-point splice vs PNG_FIXED_ERROR overflow), i2s_wrong_offset_derived_codepoint (5954: wrong-offset Thai 0x0E1B/D/F), and i2s_over_solves_true_branch_starves_false (5723/5881: I2S substitutes the CORRECT true-side operand, depleting the winner=false-side bytes). All FIVE share ONE measurable signature -- a fixed-offset cmplog-corpus DEPLETION of the winner value (signed_target_enrich < 0) with decoy enrichment -- measured identically by operand_enrichment (validated for anti: median -0.55, 88% separation). The over-solve pair (5723/5881) is NOT separable from plain decoy-substitution: in both, cmplog enriches a competing/true-side operand and depletes the winner-side bytes -- mechanically the same corpus signature -- so per the spec's 'fold unless a metric separates them' I fold it in rather than keep a 6th fuzzy hypothesis. (2) i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand (the prior 15-member cluster) stays, keyed on operand_enrichment's no_gate_signature return -- there is genuinely no input-resident operand (Ragel/table/Unicode-property-derived gates) so I2S is pure overhead. (3) i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation (5949/5969/5972) is KEPT SEPARATE from (2) ONLY because a metric DOES separate it: these are also no-operand/derived gates (they too return no_gate_signature) but their hallmark is a resolving-vs-blocking LENGTH asymmetry (cmplog shortens inputs below the multi-matra / multi-glyph accumulation threshold), captured by joint_necessity's size_lift>=1.3; without that length signal they would be indistinguishable from (2), so the decision order tries the length test BEFORE falling to the no-operand catch-all. DECISION ORDER: decoy-substitution first (most specific -- requires an actual fixed-offset operand AND negative enrichment); then sequence-accumulation (no_gate_signature AND size_lift>=1.3); then the no-operand overhead catch-all (no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3). A branch matching none (e.g. a fixed-offset operand with signed_target_enrich >= -0.3, i.e. neither clearly depleted nor enriched) is honestly left inconclusive rather than force-fit. TARGET DISTRIBUTION: harfbuzz 28, openthread 2, libpng 1. DATA-REALITY / G2 compliance: (a) operand_enrichment needs ON-DISK CORPUS -- of this shape's targets only harfbuzz and openthread have on-disk corpora; libpng does NOT, so libpng/3977 cannot be scored by operand_enrichment and falls to fallback=inconclusive unless its decisive offset is scored from the 286-rep seed sets a future seed_set variant of the tool supports (flagged for the orchestrator). (b) NO seed_lineage 'I2S event' is used -- I2S is untagged in mutation_op (data_reality); the decoy signature is read from the cmplog CORPUS byte frequencies, not from any lineage event. (c) byte_freq_ratio is used ONLY for the fixed-offset VALUE-gate subtype (decoy-substitution), where W and L seeds align at the gate offset; the sequence-accumulation subtype deliberately uses struct_size_ratio (NOT byte_freq_ratio) because its seeds are different-SIZED (the spec's own warning). (d) operand_enrichment needs >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in the DB; the 286 reps carry resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds, but any branch under that floor returns no_gate_signature for a DATA reason rather than a MECHANISM reason -- the arbiter should treat a no_gate_signature with <3 W/L seeds as inconclusive (insufficient evidence), NOT as confirmation of the no-operand hypothesis. (e) member_hint_branches are my a-priori split from gate_structure/operand_kind in the signatures; they are HINTS, not labels -- the deterministic rule over the named metrics makes the actual per-branch call. G2 holds throughout: every measurement reads the cmplog-vs-naive CORPUS / seed-set content (what the corpus reaches), never the resolve pattern that defined the shape.
